phase1.question_1: Leadership acknowledges and communicates the importance of innovation
phase1.question_2: A shared vision for a better future is formalized
phase1.question_3: An innovation audit has been conducted to identify obstacles
phase1.question_4: A budget is allocated to innovation, training, and initiatives
phase1.question_5: Entrepreneurship, empowerment, and risk-taking are part of the desired culture
phase1.question_6: Innovation ambassadors are appointed in different departments

phase2.question_1: Clear goals are set (e.g., number of ideas, revenue from innovation, etc.)
phase2.question_2: Training sessions are organized for managers and ambassadors
phase2.question_3: An internal suggestion system is in place and actively managed
phase2.question_4: Regular brainstorming sessions are held with diverse teams and a facilitator
phase2.question_5: Innovation objectives are included in individual evaluations
phase2.question_6: Innovation successes are shared and celebrated

phase3.question_1: An open innovation strategy is defined and being deployed
phase3.question_2: An incubation or experimentation unit is operational
phase3.question_3: Validation processes have been simplified to accelerate implementation
phase3.question_4: More than 2 suggestions per employee per year are implemented
phase3.question_5: A blame culture has been replaced with a culture of openness and questioning
phase3.question_6: The concept of minimum viable product (MVP) is fully integrated

phase4.question_1: An open innovation program is fully integrated and generates external innovations
phase4.question_2: New products/services represent a significant portion of revenue
phase4.question_3: Employees are encouraged to try new initiatives — and they do
phase4.question_4: Risk-taking is encouraged, failure is accepted and analyzed
phase4.question_5: More than 5 suggestions per employee per year are implemented
phase4.question_6: The company is recognized as an innovation leader and a stimulating workplace

embryonic.description: High potential but few structures in place.

development.description: A solid foundation, but further effort needed.

active.description: Many projects and practices are in place.

mature.description: A well-established culture and visible results.

form.consent: By clicking on 'Start Assessment', I consent to the collection and processing of the information I have provided for evaluation purposes.

evidence.label: Supporting Evidence
evidence.placeholder: Describe any supporting evidence or examples...

name.min: Name must be at least 3 characters
name.max: Name must be at most 50 characters

organization.min: Organization must be at least 3 characters

position.min: Position must be at least 3 characters

phone.min: Phone number must be at least 10 characters
phone.max: Phone number must be at most 15 characters

info.question_1: Briefly describe your current role and responsibilities.
info.question_2: Have you contributed to any innovation or improvement initiatives in your organization? Describe briefly.
info.question_3: What motivates you most in your current work?
info.question_4: What kind of problems or challenges do you enjoy solving?
info.question_5: What personal qualities do you think support innovation in your work?
info.question_6: What do you consider the main barrier to innovation in your environment?
info.question_7: What is your biggest strength when working in a team?
info.question_8: What is your biggest challenge or weakness in team-based or creative work?

question_2.question: Your strength in a project is…

question_4.question: In a new challenge, you first…
question_4.option_e: Identify what can be executed fast

question_5.question: Your ideal role in a project…

question_14.question: You are seen as someone who…

question_24.question: What excites you most in a new project?

comment.label: Leave a comment
comment.placeholder: Share a specific example or reflection related to your choice...

descriptions.a: You are a Strategist — you see the big picture and bring clarity to complexity. You excel at defining long-term goals, structuring ideas, and aligning teams with a shared vision. Your strength lies in turning chaos into coherent plans. In innovation teams, you bring direction and foresight.
descriptions.b: You are a Facilitator — the bridge between people and ideas. You create collaborative environments, ensure alignment, and remove barriers so innovation can flow. Your presence helps teams move forward with harmony and shared understanding.
descriptions.c: You are an Explorer — curious, experimental, and driven by discovery. You are not afraid to challenge norms and test new possibilities. In innovation, you spark creativity, question assumptions, and push teams toward bold new directions.
descriptions.d: You are a Mentor — supportive, reflective, and people-focused. You empower others, share knowledge, and create space for growth. In innovation, you nurture talent and help ideas mature into real value.
descriptions.e: You are a Realizer — action-oriented and results-driven. You take ideas and make them real, ensuring that progress happens. In innovation, you are essential for transforming concepts into concrete outcomes.
